title: Components of Springer Fibers Equal to Richardson Varieties
speaker: Martha Precup
abstract: Springer fibers are subvarieties of the flag variety parameterized by partitions. They are central objects of study in geometric representation theory. Given a partition λ, one of the key conclusions of Springer theory is that the top dimensional components of the associated Springer fiber are indexed by standard tableaux of shape λ. The structure of Springer fibers is entwined with the combinatorics of these tableaux and many open questions about the geometry of individual components remain open. In this talk, we study when components of a Springer fiber are also Richardson varieties. We give a precise characterization in terms of the associated tableaux. This answers a question raised by Springer regarding the cohomology class of each such component. This talk is based on joint work with Steven Karp.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Springer fibers and tableau-indexed components
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a nilpotent matrix in Jordan canonical form, defines the type-A Springer fiber as the variety of compatible full flags, and recalls Spaltenstein’s construction indexing its irreducible components by standard Young tableaux.

proof_stages.label: Representation-theoretic context and open component-geometry problems
proof_stages.summary: Springer fibers are placed in geometric representation theory via Springer correspondence, Robinson-Schensted, and evacuation, after which the main unresolved questions are framed as understanding component geometry and cohomology classes in a Schubert basis.

proof_stages.label: Total positivity motivation for Richardson components
proof_stages.summary: Lusztig’s description of totally positive Springer fibers as unions of positive open Richardson cells is used to observe that maximal-dimensional such cells close up to Richardson varieties that are components of the ordinary Springer fiber.

proof_stages.label: Tableau data defining candidate Richardson varieties
proof_stages.summary: For each standard tableau sigma, the talk defines permutations w_sigma and v_sigma using a reverse reading word of sigma and a reading word of its evacuation, producing the candidate Richardson variety X_{w_sigma} intersect X^{v_sigma}.

proof_stages.label: Richardson-tableau criterion for equality
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem states that the Springer component B_sigma equals the candidate Richardson variety exactly when, for every entry j, the last entry in the row above j in the restricted tableau is larger than every entry in lower rows; otherwise the candidate Richardson variety strictly contains the component and is too large.

proof_stages.label: Combinatorics and enumeration of Richardson tableaux
proof_stages.summary: The class of tableaux satisfying the criterion is examined through special shapes, a jeu-de-taquin/evacuation L-slide characterization, closure under evacuation, and a product formula enumerating Richardson tableaux of a fixed shape.

proof_stages.label: Cohomology-class consequences and further questions
proof_stages.summary: For Richardson components, their cohomology classes become products of Schubert classes, linking the characterization to existing hook formulas and prompting questions about multiplicities, generating functions, self-evacuating cases, rarity, and representation-theoretic meaning.

examples.name: Hook-shape Springer components
examples.note: Hook partitions are cited as a special case where cohomology-class formulas already exist and later as a case where every component is Richardson, suggesting computable Schubert-product expansions.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker applies the Richardson-tableau condition to hook shapes, argues the condition is vacuous there, and concludes hook components are Richardson varieties.
